mnemonics: AR = A(n) R(ecursive) Process: The 'R' stands for 'Regress' the past values onto the present.
keyPoints: An AR(p) process is defined by X_t = c + Σ(φ_i * X_{t-i}) + ε_t, where p is the order.
The key assumption is that the current value depends linearly on its own previous p values plus a stochastic shock.
Stationarity is not guaranteed and depends on the roots of the characteristic equation lying outside the unit circle.
commonMistakes: Confusing AR (Autoregressive) with MA (Moving Average) processes and their properties.
Failing to check for stationarity conditions before interpreting the model results.
Incorrectly specifying the order p, leading to underfitting or overfitting the model.
